sources.notes: Easy version of inverse minimum partition.
Official editorial develops the geometric/DP view in multiple parts; the easy version permits brute-force pieces.

annotation.summary: Recover or count partitions inverse to a minimum operation under easier constraints.
annotation.constraints: The easy version allows trying candidate split parameters with O(n^2) or similar checks.
annotation.core_idea: Represent each partition constraint as a lower envelope/geometric condition. For a fixed parameter, greedily/two-pointer compute the minimum partition pieces, and binary search or brute-force the inverse parameter. The easy version can directly evaluate all relevant breakpoints.
annotation.complexity: O(n^2) or O(n log n) depending on implementation.

solution_variants.name: bruteforce-geometric-check
solution_variants.summary: Enumerate candidate parameters and evaluate the minimum-partition predicate with a greedy/two-pointer check.

tags.evidence: The editorial frames the partition constraints as geometric envelope conditions.
Feasible parameters can be found by binary search in the easier approach.
The partition predicate is evaluated with monotone pointer movement.
